elements.name: Carbon
elements.curation_notes: Curated: 1s. NIST also tabulates a 1s surface core-level shift (SS-1s -0.95, Pate86) — not modeled. C KLL/KVV Auger values were not archive-recoverable — uncurated. Higher-BE chemical states (carbonates ~289.5+, fluorocarbons CF2/CF3) are NOT represented in the recovered NIST records — region top may need upward extension after hand-verification. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2015/2016).
elements.short_caveat: Reference is for elemental/adventitious carbon. Oxidized states (carbonates, C–F groups) sit at higher binding energy and may fall above the shaded band.

transitions.notes: Nominal is the raw NIST-evaluated value (Powe95, starred: 284.44) — the literature-verified C 1s elemental-carbon position, carrying this record's VERIFIED/curated status honestly (provenance audit, 2026-07-16). The app's own C 1s graphite charge-correction default is a SEPARATE, disclosed engineering convention (284.5 eV; see templates/index.html's cc-lit dropdown and Auto-Fit C1s Graphite logic) — chosen for internal fit-to-fit consistency, not asserted as a literature value, and intentionally does NOT flow through this reference/citation pipeline (curator decision, S. Fortier 2026-06). NIST evidence: 20 elemental-form records span 282.0-286.9 incl. the Powe95 evaluated 284.44; NIST compound records (2015 page) extend organic C 1s components to 289.22 — region spans both. Carbonate/fluorocarbon upward extension (>289.5) is a future expansion.

elements.name: Oxygen
elements.curation_notes: O KLL Auger KE is NOT curated: NIST tabulates only Auger-parameter records (AP-1s,KVV 1041.5 / 1043.9, KKMP88) whose KE decomposition is ambiguous — needs hand-verification against live SRD 20. NIST 1s records (531.2-533.0) are compound/adsorbate-hosted; the lattice-oxide low end (~529-530.5) is not represented in archive-recoverable records, hence the conservative-fallback region. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2016).
elements.short_caveat: The reference band reflects compound and adsorbed oxygen. Lattice/metal-oxide oxygen sits lower and may fall below the band.

transitions.notes: Median of 13 NIST records spanning 531.2-533.0 (KBG90, WVBM87, KKMP88, FPT88, TaYa87, KeSc90, RaDe91); no NIST-evaluated (starred) value exists for O 1s. Region floor extended below the NIST span to cover lattice metal oxides — fallback policy, not sourced data.
Single NIST record (RaDe91, 28.30 eV) — low-confidence nominal; weak line. Overlaps the U 6p1/2 region in uranium samples.

elements.name: Chlorine
elements.curation_notes: Curated: 2p doublet. The single archive-recoverable NIST elemental-page record (TPPF90) has an unidentified specimen (detail popup unarchived) — its 198.30 eV sits in metal-chloride territory; hand-check against live SRD 20. Chloride-compound 2p values were NOT archive-recoverable (the recovered compound page holds only Cl 1s records near 2815 eV from hard-X-ray work, beyond Al Ka reach) — region is fallback. Cl 2s not recovered — uncurated. In actinide chlorides (e.g. UCl4) the Cl 2p region overlaps U 5p3/2 — expect interference. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2015/2016).
elements.short_caveat: The 2p reference is a single chloride-type value of unidentified specimen — use it as an approximate guide for the chloride region.

transitions.notes: Single NIST elemental-page record (TPPF90, 198.30); splitting from the NIST DS-2p record (BDH74, 1.60). Region sized to span metal chlorides through organochlorine (fallback, unsourced); UCl4-type chlorides sit near the nominal. The narrow 1.6 eV splitting means the doublet often appears as one envelope in surveys. Overlaps U 5p3/2 in actinide chlorides.
DERIVED: 198.30 (TPPF90) + 1.60 (DS-2p, BDH74) = 199.90 — no direct NIST 2p1/2 record was archive-recoverable. Needs hand-verification.

elements.name: Copper
elements.curation_notes: Curated: 2p doublet (LMM Auger lives in auger-lines.json). NIST also tabulates 3s (122.2-122.9), 3p3/2 (75.14, Seah89 NIST-evaluated), 2s (1096.4-1097.1), DS-3p (2.2-2.39) — not curated in v1. Cu2O/CuO/Cu(OH)2 chemical-state values and the Cu(II) shake-up satellites (~940-945 eV) were not recoverable from archived NIST pages — uncurated. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2016).
elements.short_caveat: Reference is for copper metal. Cu(II) species show strong shake-up satellites near 940–945 eV that aren't included here but are diagnostic of oxidation state.

transitions.notes: NIST-evaluated value (Seah89, starred); 29 raw records span 932.2-933.1. Splitting = median of NIST DS-2p records (19.8 x3, 19.82 x2, 19.9 x2); note 952.56 - 932.67 = 19.89 disagrees slightly with the DS median — hand-check. Region top covers Cu(II) states (fallback, unsourced); area_ratio 2:1 from 2p degeneracy (2j+1).
Direct NIST records: 952.56 x2 + 952.55 (Mans94202), 952.45 (MiSi9355) — not derived from the splitting.

elements.name: Niobium
elements.curation_notes: Curated: 3d doublet. Nb-3d3/2 nominal is DERIVED (3d5/2 + DS-3d median), not a direct NIST record — the page holding direct 3d3/2 values was not archive-recoverable; hand-check. NIST also tabulates 3p3/2 (360.4-360.7), 3s (466.8-467.1), 4p3/2 (30.77, Powe95 NIST-evaluated), 4s (56.4), DS-3p (15.4-15.5), M5N23V Auger KE (167.6, NIST-evaluated) — not curated in v1. Oxide values (NbO/NbO2/Nb2O5) were not recoverable from archived NIST pages — region tops are fallback. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2016).
elements.short_caveat: Reference is for niobium metal. Oxides (Nb₂O₅, NbO₂) sit at higher binding energy; the band's upper extent for those states is approximate.

transitions.notes: NIST-evaluated value (Powe12, 2016 snapshot; the 2004 snapshot's evaluated Powe95 gives 202.34). 14 raw records span 201.5-202.6. Splitting = median of NIST DS-3d records (2.6, 2.75, 2.8 x3); handbooks often cite 2.72 — hand-check. Region top covers Nb(V) oxide (fallback, unsourced); area_ratio 3:2 from 3d degeneracy.
DERIVED: 202.31 (Powe12) + 2.8 (DS-3d median) = 205.11 — no direct NIST 3d3/2 record was archive-recoverable. Needs hand-verification against live SRD 20.
